column2: Individuals who have created an account and purchased from the business
column5: Name, email, phone, address, purchase history, payment records
column7: Checkout consent checkbox + privacy policy link
column9: Standard rights apply. Review erasure process across all systems.

column2: Individuals who completed a purchase without creating an account
column5: Name, email, phone, delivery address, order details
column9: Limited erasure possible — payment records may be under regulatory hold

column2: Individuals who subscribed to email marketing
column7: Email opt-in form / Shopify newsletter checkbox
column9: Must honour unsubscribe immediately. Review suppression list.

column2: Individuals who visited the website — tracked via analytics
column5: Anonymised IP, device type, session data
column9: Verify IP anonymisation in GA4. Implement cookie consent banner if not done.

column2: Current and former employees of the organisation
column5: Name, address, PAN, Aadhaar, bank account, salary, attendance, leave
column8: HR lead or founder direct contact
column9: HIGH SENSITIVITY — PAN and Aadhaar held. Restrict access. Minimum 5-year retention post-exit for statutory compliance.

column2: Individuals engaged on a contract or project basis
column5: Name, PAN, bank account, service agreement
column9: PAN required for TDS. Retain contract and invoices for 7 years.

column2: Individuals who applied for jobs but were not placed or hired
column5: Name, phone, email, resume, work history
column9: Review retention period — delete within 1 year if not hired and no ongoing engagement

column5: Name, work email, work phone, job title
column9: B2B contact data — lower risk but still personal data if individually identifiable

column2: Individuals who contacted support but may not be registered
column5: Name, email or phone, issue description, conversation history
column9: May overlap with registered customers. Avoid duplicate erasure gaps.

Section 11 — Data Principals have the right to request a summary of their personal data and processing activities.
Section 12 — Data Principals have the right to correction and erasure.
Section 9 — If any category above includes individuals under 18 add a row and flag it. Verifiable parental consent required.
